---
title: "masker"
generated: true
description: "Create a JIT-compatible function that applies a mask to a tensor."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.numerics.tensor_ops`](../nitrix-numerics-tensor_ops.qmd)

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix._internal.util`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:masker -->
```python
masker(mask: Bool[Array, ...], axis: int | Sequence[int], output_axis: int | None = None) -> Callable[[Shaped[Array, ...]], Shaped[Array, ...]]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:masker -->
Create a JIT-compatible function that applies a mask to a tensor.

::: {.callout-warning}
This function comes with some memory overhead. Specifically, it
closes over an integer array of the same size as the number of
`True` elements in the mask. When applying a very large mask to
a tensor, it is important to consider the trade-off between memory
and potential performance gains of JIT compilation.
::
::: {.callout-warning}
Just like any JIT-compiled function, the resulting function must be
recompiled when the shape of the input tensor changes.
::
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:masker -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `mask` | `Bool[Array, ...]` | — | — | — |
| `axis` | `int | Sequence[int]` | — | — | — |
| `output_axis` | `int | None` | — | `None` | — |

**Returns** `Callable[[Shaped[Array, ...]], Shaped[Array, ...]]`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
